people.narrative:
I used to teach at Durmstrang. Thought I could do some good. School has been a hotbed for bad wizard folk since as long as I’ve been alive. I thought if I could go there and teach those little ones early, maybe I could get through to them. Maybe a few wouldn’t turn so bad. 

 

I was a fool. It broke me. I moved out here and swore of magic altogether.
people.notes: Moved to Greenland after failing to bring good to the children at Durmstrang as the perception professor. He gave up on magic altogether. He doesn't speak to his family and they are bitter about it.

people.notes: Eldist's upbringing was marked by poverty, yet his family remained generous and permissive. However, he internalized this scarcity, associating wealth with power—a shield against the vulnerability of indigence. Exceptionally industrious and hardworking, Eldist rose to become a preeminent wandmaker. Now, he hoards his wealth and seeks greater power, driven by a desire to avert the anguish of his youth.

people.notes: Edivan’s upbringing was marred by neglect, compelling him to seek solace and companionship in the forest. The verdant expanse became his sanctuary, a place where he forged profound connections with the wildlife. He honed an acute understanding of the forest’s intricate web of life, intuitively sensing the needs and desires of its inhabitants. His neglect sowed seeds of bitterness, yet an unquenchable yearning for belonging lingered within him. The council, recognizing his unique bond with nature, extended an offer of acceptance—a means to integrate with humanity without forsaking his communion with the natural world.

imported_fields.Notes: Edivan’s upbringing was marred by neglect, compelling him to seek solace and companionship in the forest. The verdant expanse became his sanctuary, a place where he forged profound connections with the wildlife. He honed an acute understanding of the forest’s intricate web of life, intuitively sensing the needs and desires of its inhabitants. His neglect sowed seeds of bitterness, yet an unquenchable yearning for belonging lingered within him. The council, recognizing his unique bond with nature, extended an offer of acceptance—a means to integrate with humanity without forsaking his communion with the natural world.

people.notes: A man in Harry Potter's year who was known to be quite a geek in school, heading the Gobblestones club at Hogwarts. Carina withdrew him from Hogwarts prior to his seventh year, after news of Dumbledore's death. During that year's gap in schooling, Carina sought to discipline him and turn him into her vision of an upstanding citizen. He ended up going back to Hogwarts to complete his seventh year, graduating right before Kai started his first year. He now works a very lackluster Ministry job, but is rather content with living a mediocre existence. Kai views Marcus as a childish disappointment of an older cousin, and refused to interact with him during the entire year Marcus was at home.

imported_fields.Notes: A man in Harry Potter's year who was known to be quite a geek in school, heading the Gobblestones club at Hogwarts. Carina withdrew him from Hogwarts prior to his seventh year, after news of Dumbledore's death. During that year's gap in schooling, Carina sought to discipline him and turn him into her vision of an upstanding citizen. He ended up going back to Hogwarts to complete his seventh year, graduating right before Kai started his first year. He now works a very lackluster Ministry job, but is rather content with living a mediocre existence. Kai views Marcus as a childish disappointment of an older cousin, and refused to interact with him during the entire year Marcus was at home.

imported_fields.Relationship Note [1]: The person filling the mother/caretaker role for Kai. Carina takes a Mary Poppins/Alice Longbottom-type of approach when it comes to disciplining Marcus and later, Kai. She rarely praises them, and only seeks to correct and fairly discipline them when they do something wrong. She values her father's morals, emphasizing hard work and humility as key values to uphold in an upstanding person, and thus often criticizes Marcus for his laziness and interest in useless hobbies such as Gobblestones and Kai for his sheer pride, unable to understand why he seeks to restore his affiliation to the Black family. She also dislikes Norma for her lack of support in the family, and blames her as the source of Kai's pride and fascination of dueling. She'd rather have Kai grow up to be more like his father/her brother, a respected and knowledgeable, but inconspicuous, authority in the wizarding world. She fears for Kai's life, as she doesn't want Kai to suffer the same fate as Benjy. However, she feels hypocritical on this point, as she herself was known to be a strong duelist and agent of espionage as well. Her time discreetly working with the Order of the Phoenix while serving as editor at the Daily Prophet during the First Wizarding War has kept her a silent supporter of the Order in recent years. Though uninvolved, she is a very sharp linguist, able to easily notice something is wrong whenever a story in the Daily Prophet drops in quality or when a writer produces a work that is different from his/her normal work.The same can be said for changes in manner of speech over the radio or other forms of media. If she weren't a homemaker, she could easily pass for a judge in the Wizengamot, able to discern truth from fiction solely by the voice and body language of a speaker. As a result, Kai knows he cannot lie to Carina and wishes he had her ability to read people without using Legilimency.
imported_fields.Relationship Note [2]: He is one of the leading experts on death and conjuration at the Ministry, though few actually know about him due to his status as an Unspeakable.  He and Carina were taught early on by their father, Kresnik, the value of hard work and humility, so as a Ministry researcher, he dedicates the majority of his time and effort to his study of souls and creation, often working long hours voluntarily. As a result, he doesn't have much of a personable relationship with Kai and is very laissez-faire when it comes to Kai's upbringing. However, though he is very passive in demeanor, his mind is very sharp, and he often informs Carina of matters at the Ministry that could prove problematic or dangerous to their home life, often predicting investigations in time for his family to move. Kai and the family know about Perseus's research topic at the Ministry, but none of the details or experiments conducted. However, he is more approachable than Norma and more knowledgeable on certain subjects than Carina, so Kai often asks him questions about subjects other people don't seem to know about. Perseus appreciates Kai's occasional curiosity and treasures those moments the most, even when the questions are sent as letters. Ever cautious of the actions at the Ministry and among the Death Eaters, he and Kai have developed several forms of coding in their letters to each other. Much of Perseus's research involves studying the Veil in the Death Chamber and its relationship to ghosts, poltergeists, and the recently departed; although recently, he has started working with his wife to track specific souls as they travel across time and space. Although his wife has been criticized by her colleagues, Perseus is well-respected among his colleagues, due to his long and dedicated tenure in the department.
